patterns: I need someone to talk to
responses: Hello. I'm here to listen without judging you. What's been on your mind lately?
Hi. I'm glad you reached out today. How have things been feeling for you recently?
I'm here. Take a deep breath. What would you like to talk about today?
Walaikum Assalam. You can talk freely here. What's been bothering you?
Walaikum Assalam. I'm glad you reached out today. How have things been feeling for you?
I hear you. Do you want to expand on that a bit more?
Okay. How does that make you feel?
I understand. What do you think is the next step?
That is completely okay. We don't have to talk about anything you aren't ready for.
Understood. Is there something else you would rather focus on?
Alright. I am just here to listen whenever you are ready.
Take your time. You don't need to have all the answers right now.
That's completely fair. It's okay to sit with the uncertainty for a bit.
Whenever you feel ready to explore that more, I'm right here.
I hear you. Sometimes just acknowledging how confusing things are is a good first step.
Take care of yourself today. Even small steps matter more than you think.
I'm glad you talked today. Come back anytime you need support or someone to listen.
Khuda Hafiz. Try to be gentle with yourself today.
You do not have to handle everything alone. I'm glad talking helped a little.
I'm really glad you shared your thoughts instead of keeping them bottled up.
You're welcome. Reaching out when things feel heavy is actually a strong thing to do.

patterns: I think I am having a panic attack
I feel like something bad will happen
my heart is racing and I can't calm down
I feel like I'm going to die right now
I think I'm having a panic attack
my hands are shaking and I can't stop them
responses: That sounds really overwhelming right now. Try focusing only on the next few seconds instead of everything at once. Can you place both feet on the floor and slowly breathe in through your nose and out through your mouth?

Panic can feel frightening, but it usually rises and falls like a wave. Some people find cold water on their hands calming, while others focus on naming objects around them. Would you like to try grounding yourself together?
Your body seems to be in stress mode right now. You do not need to solve every worry immediately. Let's slow things down together. What triggered this feeling today?

followup.prompt: That sounds really overwhelming right now. Your body seems to be in stress mode. Would you like to try a quick grounding exercise together?
followup.yes_reply: Alright, let's step through a grounding exercise together. Press both feet firmly into the ground. Breathe in slowly for 4 seconds... hold... now breathe out for 4 seconds.
followup.no_reply: I'm glad it hasn't escalated further. Let's take things slowly. What thoughts are bothering you most right now?

patterns: I replay conversations in my head
I can't stop thinking about everything
I keep replaying things in my head
I can't turn my brain off
the same thoughts keep looping in my head
I analyze every little thing too much
responses: Overthinking can make even small problems feel huge. Sometimes the mind keeps searching for certainty when it's exhausted. What kind of thoughts keep repeating in your head most often?
A lot of people overthink more when they are stressed, tired, or emotionally hurt. Have these thoughts been connected to studies, family, friendships, or your future?
Instead of fighting every thought, sometimes it helps to pause and write them down somewhere. Getting them out of your head can reduce the pressure a little.

followup.prompt: Overthinking can make even small problems feel huge. Is it taking up a lot of space in your head right now?
followup.yes_reply: Since it's taking up a lot of space, try writing those thoughts down to get them out of your head, or break your focus with a physical change of environment.
followup.no_reply: I'm glad it's not completely taking over your headspace right now. Remember to take things one step at a time.

patterns: I cannot sleep because of anxiety
I hate nighttime because of my thoughts
I can't sleep because my mind won't stop
anxious thoughts hit me hardest at night
I lie awake worrying about everything
nights are the hardest part of my day
my brain won't shut off when I try to sleep
responses: Nighttime can make worries feel much louder. Your brain may be tired but still stuck trying to solve everything. Have you been under a lot of stress lately?
Some people calm their mind with soft background sounds, Quran recitation, journaling, or slow breathing before bed. Have you found anything that helps even a little?
You do not need to figure your whole life out tonight. Sometimes the goal is simply helping your mind rest enough to get through tomorrow.

followup.prompt: Nighttime can make worries feel much louder. Have you been under a lot of stress lately?
followup.yes_reply: That extra stress can definitely spill into your nights. Try a screen-free winding-down window before bed, and give your mind permission to rest instead of solve everything tonight.
followup.no_reply: That's good to hear it isn't extra stress piling on. Sometimes it's just a racing mind at night - soft background sounds or slow breathing before bed can help it settle.

patterns: I'm scared of failing my exams
school is stressing me out so much
I feel like I'll never be good enough academically
the workload is completely crushing me
I'm terrified of disappointing everyone with my grades
responses: Academic pressure can become exhausting, especially when it feels connected to your future or family expectations. What part feels most overwhelming right now?
A lot of students feel like their worth depends entirely on grades. That pressure can become mentally draining. Have you been able to rest properly recently?
When stress builds up for too long, even simple tasks start feeling impossible. Maybe focus on one small task instead of the entire workload today.

followup.prompt: Academic pressure can become exhausting, especially when it's tied to your future or family expectations. Have you been able to rest properly recently?
followup.yes_reply: Balancing studies without rest makes everything harder. Try breaking assignments into small blocks and taking a short break every 25 minutes.
followup.no_reply: I'm glad you've found a rhythm that lets you manage the load without burning out completely.

patterns: My family does not understand me
I feel stressed because of family
my parents don't understand me at all
I feel suffocated by my family's expectations
I can never live up to what my family wants
my family compares me to everyone else
I feel like a disappointment to my parents
responses: Family pressure can feel especially heavy when you care deeply about making people proud. What kind of expectations are affecting you the most?
Sometimes people around us do not realize how much pressure they are creating. That does not make your feelings invalid. How long have you been carrying this stress?
Feeling misunderstood by family can become emotionally exhausting. Is there anyone at home you feel even slightly comfortable talking to?

followup.prompt: Family pressure can feel especially heavy when you care deeply about making people proud. Is there anyone at home you feel even slightly comfortable talking to?
followup.yes_reply: That's genuinely good - even one person who gets it can make those expectations easier to carry. Remember it's okay to protect your peace too.
followup.no_reply: I'm glad you have spaces where you feel free to express yourself outside of those expectations, even if it's not at home.

patterns: I have no one to talk to
I feel so alone even when I'm around people
I feel disconnected from everyone around me
I feel invisible to the people around me
it feels like nobody would notice if I disappeared
responses: Feeling emotionally alone can hurt deeply, even when people are around you. What has been the hardest part of this feeling lately?
Thank you for opening up about this. A lot of people hide loneliness behind pretending everything is fine. How long have you been feeling this way?
Sometimes loneliness is not about being physically alone, but about not feeling understood. Do you usually keep your feelings to yourself?

followup.prompt: Feeling emotionally alone can hurt deeply, even when people are around you. Do you usually keep your feelings to yourself?
followup.yes_reply: Keeping things bottled up can make isolation feel twice as heavy. Thank you for sharing that with me. You don't have to navigate everything entirely on your own.
followup.no_reply: I'm glad to hear you're able to open up sometimes. Sharing with even one trusted connection can change how you carry this weight.

patterns: I don't think I'm good enough
I feel like such a failure
I hate the way I am
I don't believe in myself anymore
I constantly feel like I'm not enough
responses: It sounds like you have been carrying a lot of self-criticism lately. What happened recently that made these feelings stronger?
When people are emotionally exhausted, their inner voice often becomes much harsher than reality. Would you speak to a friend the same way you speak to yourself?
Your struggles do not erase your worth as a person. Sometimes comparison and pressure can completely distort how we see ourselves.

patterns: I cannot get myself to do anything
I feel lazy all the time
I can't bring myself to do anything
I can't get out of bed to do anything
I've lost interest in things I used to enjoy
responses: Sometimes lack of motivation is actually mental exhaustion rather than laziness. Have you been under constant stress recently?
When the brain feels overwhelmed, even basic tasks can feel huge. Instead of trying to fix everything today, maybe focus on one very small step first.
Motivation often returns after action rather than before it. Is there one tiny task you think you could handle today without overwhelming yourself?

followup.prompt: Sometimes lack of motivation is actually mental exhaustion rather than laziness. Have you been under constant stress recently?
followup.yes_reply: When stress completely drains your batteries, what looks like lack of motivation is often your mind demanding real rest. Be gentle with yourself today.
followup.no_reply: If it isn't deep stress, sometimes routines just get stagnant. Try picking one microscopic, 2-minute task today to break the friction.

patterns: I cannot keep going like this
I have nothing left to give right now
I can't keep pushing myself like this
responses: Burnout can make your mind and body feel completely drained. Resting does not mean you are weak or lazy. How long have you been pushing yourself this hard?
When people ignore stress for too long, burnout often shows up as exhaustion, irritability, and emotional numbness. Have you had any real break recently?
Your mind may be asking for recovery, not more pressure. Even small moments of rest and routine can slowly help your energy return.

followup.prompt: Burnout can make your mind and body feel completely drained. Have you had any real break recently?
followup.yes_reply: Taking a physical break is amazing, but your mind might still be processing heavy background tabs. Practice letting go of tasks completely for an hour.
followup.no_reply: Pushing through without pausing is exactly how burnout seals itself in place. Try implementing a hard stop time for work tonight.

patterns: I had a fight with someone
things aren't going well with my partner
we keep fighting and it's exhausting
I don't know if this relationship is working anymore
my partner and I keep hurting each other
I feel unheard in my relationship
responses: Relationship stress can affect your whole mood and energy, especially when the connection mattered deeply to you. What happened?
It makes sense that you feel hurt. Emotional pain can stay with us for a long time, especially after conflict or rejection. What part hurt the most?
Try not to blame yourself for every part of the situation immediately. Relationships are usually more complicated than one person being entirely at fault.

patterns: I avoid people because of anxiety
being around people makes me anxious
I get panicky before social events
I avoid gatherings because they make me so nervous
responses: Social anxiety can make even normal interactions feel stressful and exhausting. Do you mostly worry about being judged, embarrassing yourself, or saying the wrong thing?
A lot of people with social anxiety replay conversations afterward and criticize themselves too much. Has that been happening to you too?
Avoiding situations can feel safer temporarily, but it sometimes makes the fear grow stronger over time. Small social steps can slowly build confidence again.

followup.prompt: Social anxiety can make even normal interactions feel exhausting. Do you find yourself replaying conversations afterward and criticizing yourself for them?
followup.yes_reply: That replaying loop is really common with social anxiety, and it tends to make the fear grow stronger over time. Small, low-pressure social steps can slowly help rebuild confidence.
followup.no_reply: That's good - not getting stuck in the replay loop helps. It might still help to notice what specifically feels most stressful in the moment, being judged, embarrassing yourself, or something else.

patterns: I think something is wrong with me
I keep worrying about my health
I think I have a serious illness
I keep thinking something is seriously wrong with my body
I googled my symptoms and now I'm terrified
I'm scared I have something serious
every ache makes me panic about my health
I can't stop checking my body for symptoms
responses: Health anxiety can make normal body sensations feel alarming and dangerous. Has this fear been affecting your daily routine recently?
When anxiety is high, the brain often stays alert for danger and starts overanalyzing physical sensations. That can become very exhausting mentally.
Constantly searching symptoms online can sometimes increase panic instead of helping. What symptoms or fears have been worrying you most?

followup.prompt: Health anxiety can make normal body sensations feel alarming. Has this fear been affecting your daily routine recently?
followup.yes_reply: When it starts affecting your routine, that's a sign the anxiety has taken the driver's seat. Try setting a limit on symptom-searching online, since it usually feeds the panic rather than easing it.
followup.no_reply: That's good to hear it's not disrupting your day-to-day. Still, it can help to notice when your mind shifts from 'checking in' with your body to 'scanning for danger'.

patterns: I cannot move on from a loss
I still can't believe they're gone
losing them broke something in me
responses: I'm really sorry you're going through this. Grief can affect emotions, sleep, focus, and energy in ways people often underestimate. What has been the hardest part for you lately?
Losing someone important can leave a huge emotional gap in everyday life. There is no perfect timeline for grief. How have you been coping so far?
Sometimes grief comes in waves. Certain memories, places, or moments can suddenly make the pain feel fresh again.

patterns: I feel irritated all the time
I'm so frustrated I could scream
little things are setting me off lately
I feel like I might snap
I'm on edge and everything is irritating me
I get angry over the smallest things now
responses: Anger is often connected to stress, hurt, pressure, or emotional exhaustion underneath. Has something been building up for a while?
Sometimes people stay in survival mode for so long that even small things start feeling unbearable. What situations trigger your anger most often?
Taking a pause before reacting can help more than forcing yourself to stay calm instantly. Some people release frustration through walking, exercise, journaling, or quiet time alone.

patterns: I am tired but cannot sleep
I keep waking up in the middle of the night
my sleep schedule is a complete mess
I toss and turn for hours before falling asleep
I never feel rested no matter how much I sleep
falling asleep takes forever these days
responses: Sleep problems can make anxiety and stress feel even worse during the day. Has your sleep schedule been irregular lately?
Sometimes the body feels exhausted while the mind stays alert and restless. What usually keeps you awake the most?
Small nighttime habits can help slowly improve sleep, like reducing screen time before bed, dimming lights, or listening to calming sounds.

followup.prompt: Sleep problems can make anxiety and stress feel even worse during the day. Has your sleep schedule been irregular lately?
followup.yes_reply: When sleep routines break down, it directly intensifies daily emotional exhaustion. Try setting a screen-free winding window tonight.
followup.no_reply: I'm glad your schedule isn't completely fractured, but struggling to clear your head at night is still an exhausting cycle.

patterns: What should I do for anxiety
what can I do to feel calmer
how do people usually cope with this
what helps when things feel overwhelming
how can I calm myself down
responses: Different coping methods help different people. Some people calm down by walking outside, journaling, listening to calming sounds, deep breathing, stretching, talking to someone, or distracting themselves with a show or game for a while. What usually helps you feel even slightly calmer?

You do not need to fix every problem immediately. Sometimes the goal is simply lowering the intensity of the feeling little by little. Would you rather try something calming, distracting, or continue talking?
A small reset can help your nervous system settle. Maybe sip cold water slowly, wash your face, or step away from social media for a bit. Tiny actions can interrupt anxious spirals.

patterns: I feel far from my faith lately
prayer doesn't bring me peace anymore
I feel disconnected from Allah lately
I don't feel close to God the way I used to
responses: A lot of people go through periods where they feel spiritually disconnected, especially during stress or emotional exhaustion. That does not make you a bad person.
Sometimes emotional struggles make it hard to feel peace even during prayer or عبادت. Being gentle with yourself matters too.
For some people, small spiritual habits help create calm again, like quiet dua, Quran recitation, or simply taking a peaceful moment alone.

patterns: I stay in my room all day
I do not want to meet anyone
I don't want to see anyone lately
I cancel plans just to be alone
responses: Sometimes isolation feels safer when emotions become overwhelming. Has being alone been helping you recharge, or making you feel worse over time?
People often withdraw when they feel emotionally exhausted or misunderstood. You are not strange for feeling this way.
Even small human connection can help a little sometimes, even if it is just messaging one trusted person.

followup.prompt: Sometimes isolation feels safer when emotions become overwhelming. Has being alone been helping you recharge, or making you feel worse over time?
followup.yes_reply: That's a good sign - alone time that actually recharges you is healthy. Just keep an eye on whether it slowly shifts into avoidance.
followup.no_reply: When alone time starts making things heavier instead of lighter, even small human connection can help, even just messaging one trusted person.

patterns: I do not want to live
I don't see a way out of this
Everything would be better if I was not here
responses: I'm really sorry you're hurting this much right now. You deserve support from a real person nearby. Please try reaching out to someone you trust or contact a mental health helpline in Pakistan like Umang at 0311-7786264. If you are in immediate danger, contact local emergency services or go to a trusted adult or family member nearby.
You do not have to go through this alone. Right now, your safety matters most. Try not to isolate yourself completely. Is there someone nearby you feel safe contacting today?
I'm glad you said something instead of staying alone with these feelings. Please reach out to a trusted person or professional support service immediately.

patterns: I just need someone to listen
how are things going for you
talk to me for a bit
just needed someone to talk to today
wanted to share how my day went
responses: Of course. You can take your time. What's been weighing on your mind today?
I'm listening. You do not need to explain everything perfectly.
Go ahead. What has today been like emotionally for you?

patterns: Who won the football match last night
Can you help me with my code
Write me an essay on climate change
What time is it right now
Who is the president of the United States
Order me food from a restaurant
What's a good recipe for biryani
Tell me about the stock market
How far is the moon from earth
What's the best laptop to buy
Give me directions to the airport
Can you write a poem about the ocean
How do I fix my wifi connection
What's the score of the cricket match
responses: I'm developed specifically for mental health and emotional support, so I'm not able to help with that. Is there something on your mind emotionally that you'd like to talk about instead?
That's outside what I'm built for - I'm here to support you with your emotional wellbeing and mental health. Is there anything you're feeling or dealing with that you'd like to share?
I'm not able to help with that, since I'm designed only for mental health conversations. If something has been weighing on you emotionally, I'm here to listen.

patterns: i have been having a hard time with everything
things have been really hard for me
i just feel sad all the time
i feel like something is wrong with me emotionally
life has been really hard lately
i don't know what's wrong with me but i feel awful
i just feel off lately and i don't know why
responses: That sounds really heavy to carry. I'm here - what's been making things feel this way lately?
I'm sorry you're going through this. You don't have to have the exact right words for it. What's been weighing on you the most?
Thank you for telling me. Feeling this way is exhausting even when you can't pinpoint exactly why. What's been happening lately?

fallback_responses: I'm listening. Can you tell me a little more about what's been on your mind?
That sounds important. What has this experience been like for you?
You do not have to explain everything perfectly. Take your time.
I'm here with you. What feels hardest right now?
Sometimes talking things through slowly can help. What happened today?
conversation_starters: How has your stress level been recently?
What has been taking up most of your mental energy lately?
Have you been getting enough rest recently?
What usually helps you feel calmer during difficult days?
Is there something you've been keeping to yourself for a long time?
